context: Machine learning is a subset of artificial intelligence.
answer: Machine learning is a subset of AI.

context: Neural networks are computing systems inspired by biological neurons.
Gradient descent is an optimization algorithm.
Overfitting occurs when a model learns noise in training data.
Transformers use self-attention mechanisms.
Retrieval augmented generation combines search with LLMs.
Embeddings are dense vector representations of text.
Cosine similarity measures angle between vectors.
BM25 is a ranking function for text retrieval.
Fine-tuning adapts a pre-trained model to specific tasks.
A knowledge base stores curated domain content.
answer: A knowledge base stores domain content.

context: Tokenization splits text into subword units.
Prompt engineering designs effective LLM inputs.
Hallucination is generating plausible but incorrect information.
Chunking splits documents into smaller segments for retrieval.
Vector databases store and query high-dimensional embeddings.
Re-ranking reorders initial retrieval results for better relevance.
Confidence scores indicate answer reliability.
Server-sent events enable real-time streaming to clients.
Role-based access control assigns permissions by role.
